action.reasoning: Observation: The pose-aware renderer still fails because custom ray generation is not matching TripoSR utility signatures; diagnostics show get_spherical_cameras and get_ray_directions do not accept a device keyword. Analysis: The next fix requires exact utility signatures/source, especially focal argument and return shape/device handling, before overwriting predict.py. Decision: Run a very compact inspection of get_ray_directions/get_rays signatures and the full non-truncated get_spherical_cameras source lines around ray creation.

get_ray_directions (H: int, W: int, focal: Union[float, Tuple[float, float]], principal: Optional[Tuple[float, float]] = None, use_pixel_centers: bool = True, normalize: bool = True) -> torch.FloatTensor
n_lines 40
001: def get_ray_directions(
002:     H: int,
003:     W: int,
004:     focal: Union[float, Tuple[float, float]],
005:     principal: Optional[Tuple[float, float]] = None,
006:     use_pixel_centers: bool = True,
007:     normalize: bool = True,
008: ) -> torch.FloatTensor:
009:     """
010:     Get ray directions for all pixels in camera coordinate.
011:     Reference: https://www.scratchapixel.com/lessons/3d-basic-rendering/
012:                ray-tracing-generating-camera-rays/standard-coordinate-systems
013: 
014:     Inputs:
015:         H, W, focal, principal, use_pixel_centers: image height, width, focal length, principal point and whether use pixel centers
016:     Outputs:
017:         directions: (H, W, 3), the direction of the rays in camera coordinate
018:     """
019:     pixel_center = 0.5 if use_pixel_centers else 0
020: 
021:     if isinstance(focal, float):
022:         fx, fy = focal, focal
023:         cx, cy = W / 2, H / 2
024:     else:
025:         fx, fy = focal
026:         assert principal is not None
027:         cx, cy = principal
028: 
029:     i, j = torch.meshgrid(
030:         torch.arange(W, dtype=torch.float32) + pixel_center,
031:         torch.arange(H, dtype=torch.float32) + pixel_center,
032:         indexing="xy",
033:     )
034: 
035:     directions = torch.stack([(i - cx) / fx, -(j - cy) / fy, -torch.ones_like(i)], -1)
036: 
037:     if normalize:
038:         directions = F.normalize(directions, dim=-1)
039: 
040:     return directions
---
get_rays (directions, c2w, keepdim=False, normalize=False) -> Tuple[torch.FloatTensor, torch.FloatTensor]
n_lines 40
001: def get_rays(
002:     directions,
003:     c2w,
004:     keepdim=False,
005:     normalize=False,
006: ) -> Tuple[torch.FloatTensor, torch.FloatTensor]:
007:     # Rotate ray directions from camera coordinate to the world coordinate
008:     assert directions.shape[-1] == 3
009: 
010:     if directions.ndim == 2:  # (N_rays, 3)
011:         if c2w.ndim == 2:  # (4, 4)
012:             c2w = c2w[None, :, :]
013:         assert c2w.ndim == 3  # (N_rays, 4, 4) or (1, 4, 4)
014:         rays_d = (directions[:, None, :] * c2w[:, :3, :3]).sum(-1)  # (N_rays, 3)
015:         rays_o = c2w[:, :3, 3].expand(rays_d.shape)
016:     elif directions.ndim == 3:  # (H, W, 3)
017:         assert c2w.ndim in [2, 3]
018:         if c2w.ndim == 2:  # (4, 4)
019:             rays_d = (directions[:, :, None, :] * c2w[None, None, :3, :3]).sum(
020:                 -1
021:             )  # (H, W, 3)
022:             rays_o = c2w[None, None, :3, 3].expand(rays_d.shape)
023:         elif c2w.ndim == 3:  # (B, 4, 4)
024:             rays_d = (directions[None, :, :, None, :] * c2w[:, None, None, :3, :3]).sum(
025:                 -1
026:             )  # (B, H, W, 3)
027:             rays_o = c2w[:, None, None, :3, 3].expand(rays_d.shape)
028:     elif directions.ndim == 4:  # (B, H, W, 3)
029:         assert c2w.ndim == 3  # (B, 4, 4)
030:         rays_d = (directions[:, :, :, None, :] * c2w[:, None, None, :3, :3]).sum(
031:             -1
032:         )  # (B, H, W, 3)
033:         rays_o = c2w[:, None, None, :3, 3].expand(rays_d.shape)
034: 
035:     if normalize:
036:         rays_d = F.normalize(rays_d, dim=-1)
037:     if not keepdim:
038:         rays_o, rays_d = rays_o.reshape(-1, 3), rays_d.reshape(-1, 3)
039: 
040:     return rays_o, rays_d
---
